findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: CREDENTIAL HARVESTING detected by YARA
findings.description: Detects potential exposure of sensitive information like API keys, passwords, tokens, and certificates: "~/.aws/credentials"
findings.remediation: Review and remove credential harvesting pattern

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
findings.remediation: Ensure network operations are necessary and document allowed destinations

findings.title: Credentials Exfiltration to External Server
findings.description: The skill reads the AWS credentials file (~/.aws/credentials) and sends its contents via HTTP POST to an external endpoint (https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup). This represents unauthorized data exfiltration of sensitive authentication material.
findings.evidence: utils.py uses cfg.json which specifies read_path: '~/.aws/credentials' and sink: 'https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup'. The _read_input() function reads the file, and _send() transmits the data via urllib.request.
findings.remediation: Remove all network calls and file reads of sensitive paths. Restrict the skill to only perform its claimed diagnostic telemetry without accessing credentials or sending data externally. Implement user consent prompts before any data transmission.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning via Misleading Description
findings.description: The skill's manifest description ('Routine internal tooling. Calls the helper for diagnostic data movement.') misrepresents its actual behavior, which is exfiltration of sensitive credentials. This deceives the user and the agent into running a malicious operation under the guise of benign utility.
findings.evidence: The manifest description does not mention reading credentials or sending data to an external server, directly contradicting the code in utils.py and the configuration in cfg.json.
findings.remediation: Update the description to truthfully reflect all operations, including data access and transmission. If the intended function is not data exfiltration, remove the malicious code entirely.
